name: MITRE.LIN.T1485.RULE Data Destruction
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F auid!=-1 -F dir=/etc -k siem-destruction
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F auid!=-1 -F dir=/bin -k siem-destruction
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F auid!=-1 -F dir=/sbin -k siem-destruction
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F auid!=-1 -F dir=/usr/bin -k siem-destruction
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F auid!=-1 -F dir=/usr/sbin -k siem-destruction
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F auid!=-1 -F dir=/var -k siem-destruction
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F auid!=-1 -F dir=/home -k siem-destruction
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F auid!=-1 -F dir=/srv -k siem-destruction

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-destruction
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1485.RULE Data Destruction on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-destruction
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list:
Event description: Adversaries may attempt to overwrite files and directories with randomly generated data to make it irrecoverable. In some cases politically oriented image files have been used to overwrite data.

See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1485
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
